impact: This advances PP&D daemon supervision by proving syntax-style repeat failures constrain the next prompt to one syntax-valid file repair instead of reopening broad domain contract edits.
summary: Add a tiny deterministic daemon recovery fixture and validator for repeated parser/compile failures.
target_task: Task checkbox-128: Task checkbox-128: Add a tiny fixture proving repeated Python SyntaxError, py_compile, TS1005, TS1109, or TS1128 failures convert the next daemon prompt into a one-file repair instruction rather than a broad domain contract rewrite.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
